title: Game Modes & Platform Expansion
summary: Day 33 adds new game modes (Discovery Levels, Multi-Phase Levels), resolves the dual-aesthetic tension, adds a Simplified Visual accessibility mode, introduces Variable Canvas levels with pre-placed elements, redesigns mobile interaction with bottom sheets, adds gamepad support, cross-device progress sync via export/import codes, an alternative dropdown-based wire connection for motor-impaired players, and a cold-start re-engagement system for lapsed players.

tasks.description: Discovery Levels — Open-ended levels with full gate palette and no gate budget constraint. Add 3 discovery levels (IDs 33-35) in a new Chapter 8: Discovery Lab. Players can use any approach; stars based on elegance rather than minimum gates.
acceptance_criteria: 3 new discovery levels exist with id 33, 34, 35
New chapter 'Discovery Lab' contains these levels
availableGates includes all 6 gate types
optimalGates thresholds are generous (allowing creative solutions)
Each level has a unique truth table and description emphasizing creativity

tasks.description: Multi-Phase Levels — Levels that evolve mid-solve. After completing phase 1, new requirements appear that build on the existing circuit. Add 2 multi-phase levels (IDs 36-37) in the Discovery Lab chapter. Implement phase tracking in GameState and UI updates when phase advances.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-phase levels have a 'phases' array in their definition
After solving phase 1, the truth table updates with new requirements
Player's existing circuit is preserved between phases
UI shows current phase indicator (Phase 1/2)
Stars awarded based on final phase completion

tasks.description: Dual-Aesthetic Tension Resolution — Warm up the chrome UI elements to match the breadboard aesthetic. Add amber/warm highlights to buttons, modals, and panel borders. Unify visual temperature across canvas and UI.
acceptance_criteria: Button borders and glows use warm amber tones (#D4A04A or similar)
Modal borders shift from cool blue/green to warm copper/amber
Panel backgrounds get subtle warm undertone
Overall visual temperature feels unified between canvas and chrome

tasks.description: Simplified Visual Cognitive Accessibility Mode — Add a toggle in settings for reduced visual complexity: fewer animations, higher contrast, larger text, simplified gate graphics. Uses CSS class on body.
acceptance_criteria: Settings row has 'Simplified Visual' toggle button
When active, body gets 'simplified-visual' class
Animations are reduced/disabled in simplified mode
Contrast is increased, text is larger
Setting persists in localStorage

tasks.description: Variable Canvas / Spatial Variety — Some levels start with pre-placed gates or restricted zones. Add support for 'preplacedGates' array in level definitions. Discovery levels use this feature.
acceptance_criteria: Level definitions can include 'preplacedGates' array
Pre-placed gates appear on level load and cannot be removed
Pre-placed gates are visually distinct (locked indicator)
At least one discovery level uses pre-placed gates

tasks.description: Mobile-Native Interaction Redesign — Add bottom sheet for controls on mobile, gesture-based gate selection via swipe on toolbox, thumb-zone optimized layout. Rework mobile CSS for bottom-sheet panel.
acceptance_criteria: On mobile (<768px), info panel renders as a bottom sheet
Bottom sheet has drag handle for resize
Toolbox gates are accessible via horizontal scroll at bottom
Run button is positioned in thumb-reach zone
Desktop layout is unchanged

tasks.description: Gamepad / Controller Input Support — Add Gamepad API support: D-pad navigates grid, face buttons for place/connect/delete, shoulder buttons for undo/redo. Show gamepad indicator when connected.
acceptance_criteria: Gamepad connection is detected and logged
D-pad moves a cursor overlay on the canvas
A button places/selects, B button deletes
LB/RB for undo/redo
Gamepad indicator appears in status bar when connected

tasks.description: Cross-Device Progress Sync — Export progress as a compact code string and import on another device. Add Export/Import buttons to settings area on level select.
acceptance_criteria: Export button generates a base64-encoded progress string
Import button accepts a code and restores progress
Code includes level completion, stars, streaks
Invalid codes show error message
Buttons appear in settings row on level select

tasks.description: Alternative Dropdown-Based Wire Connection — For motor-impaired players, add an accessibility wire-drawing mode: select source from dropdown, select destination from dropdown, click Connect. Toggle in settings.
acceptance_criteria: Settings has 'Accessible Wiring' toggle
When active, a wire panel appears with source/destination dropdowns
Dropdowns list all available output pins and input pins
Connect button creates the wire
Existing wire-drawing still works alongside dropdown mode

tasks.description: Cold-Start / Lapsed Player Re-Engagement — If the PWA detects a returning player after 7+ days, show a welcome-back modal with context. Track last visit date.
acceptance_criteria: Last visit date is stored in localStorage
On return after 7+ days, a welcome-back modal appears
Modal shows where player left off and a quick refresher
Modal has 'Continue' and 'Start Fresh' options
Regular visits update the last-visit timestamp

testing_focus: Discovery levels load and are playable with all gates
Multi-phase levels advance correctly after phase 1 solve
CSS warm-up doesn't break existing theme/dark mode
Pre-placed gates render and can't be deleted
Mobile bottom sheet doesn't break desktop layout
Gamepad polling doesn't cause performance issues
Welcome-back modal only shows for 7+ day gap
rollback_criteria: If any P0 bug is introduced, revert to previous commit
